output:
Batch 2 execution complete. T2 (external_media_resolver.py) and T5 (normalize_media_ref external kind) implemented and verified. All 70 relevant tests pass. One pre-existing failure from prior batch T3 (test_acquire_topic_editor_run_expires_stale_running_lease, expects 'failed' but gets 'expired') is unrelated to our changes.

T2: Created src/features/summarising/external_media_resolver.py with ExternalMediaResolver class featuring:
- 10-value ResolveOutcome enum (cache_hit, downloaded, skipped_domain, fallback_only_platform, metadata_failed, download_failed, oversize, unsupported_content_type, not_discord_upload_compatible, timed_out)
- Two-tier safety: source-provenance check + platform-policy respect
- Injectable side-effectful functions (yt-dlp, download, cache) for testability
- Streaming byte-limit enforcement during download
- Configurable byte cap (defaults to 25 MiB Discord bot limit)
- URL-key + content-hash file paths
- Sanitised logging via T1 helpers
- create_resolver() factory function
- Added yt-dlp>=2024.0.0 to requirements.txt

T5: Extended normalize_media_ref to accept kind='external':
- Updated docstring, valid kinds tuple, error message
- Updated prompt text (line 113) to include external refs explanation
- Updated tool schema enum (line 263) to include 'external'
- Added description to media_refs schema explaining external refs
- Updated test_rejects_invalid_kind error match string
- SC5 manually verified: external passes through, attachment shorthand preserved, default behavior unchanged
commands_run: python -m pytest tests/test_live_runtime_wiring.py tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py tests/test_topic_editor_media_understanding.py -v --tb=short (67 passed, 1 pre-existing failure)
python -c import verification for T2 and T5/SC5
deviations: test_acquire_topic_editor_run_expires_stale_running_lease fails with 'expired' vs expected 'failed' — pre-existing from prior batch T3, not caused by T2/T5
Advisory quality: src/features/summarising/external_media_resolver.py adds unused imports: MEDIA_CONTENT_TYPES, PLATFORM_POLICY_SHORT_FORM, hashlib, is_safelisted.

Auto-attributed 2 unclaimed file(s) to task T2 (worker reported empty files_changed): .megaplan/plans/implement-best-effort-20260514-1615/execution_batch_2.json, src/features/summarising/external_media_resolver.py

task_updates.executor_notes: Created src/features/summarising/external_media_resolver.py with ExternalMediaResolver class. Two-tier safety: (1) source-provenance check using T1 safelist before yt-dlp, (2) platform-policy respect (skip yt-dlp for fallback-link-only domains). Uses injectable side-effectful functions. 10-value ResolveOutcome enum. Streaming byte-limit enforcement during download. Configurable byte cap defaults to 25 MiB. File paths use URL-key+content-hash format. Sanitised logging via T1 helpers. Added yt-dlp to requirements.txt. All side-effectful functions are injectable.

Extended normalize_media_ref to accept kind='external'. Updated docstring, valid kind tuple to ('attachment', 'embed', 'external'), error message, prompt text (line 113), tool schema enum (line 263), added description to media_refs schema. Updated test_rejects_invalid_kind. All 54 test_topic_editor_core tests pass. SC5 verified: external passes through, shorthand unchanged, default attachment unchanged, invalid kind still raises correctly.

sense_check_acknowledgments.verdict: ACKNOWLEDGED. The resolver rejects non-image/non-video content types at step 6 of resolve() via is_discord_compatible_content_type() check. Returns ResolverResult with outcome=UNSUPPORTED_CONTENT_TYPE (structured enum, not exception) with failure_reason containing the actual content type.

VERIFIED. normalize_media_ref({'message_id': '123', 'kind': 'external', 'index': 0}) returns {'message_id': '123', 'kind': 'external', 'index': 0}. normalize_media_ref({'message_id': '123', 'attachment_index': 0}) returns {'message_id': '123', 'kind': 'attachment', 'index': 0}. Default and error behaviors preserved.
